reasoning: A productized service is a credible first revenue path because image editing is already a paid category and the workflow can be delivered manually before SaaS.
known: Paid ecommerce photo editing services exist.
OpenAI image editing can support the production workflow technically.
Mock paid-intent suggests sellers may pay for a guaranteed image pack, but this is not real validation.
assumed: The buyer is the seller/operator, not an internal designer.
A per-SKU pack is easier to sell than a subscription before repeat usage is proven.
Compliance review and revision speed are stronger differentiators than pure image quality.
toValidate: What price will real sellers pay per SKU pack?
How many SKUs does a typical target seller update per month?
Will sellers share original product photos and platform credentials safely?
Do sellers care more about Amazon compliance, ad creative, or marketplace search thumbnail CTR?
concerns: Competing with low-cost design labor and AI tools can compress pricing.
If sellers only need one-off cleanup, subscription retention may be weak.
Compliance guarantee creates liability unless bounded carefully.
nextSteps: Sell a manual SKU image pack before building self-serve automation.
Package one SKU as 1 main image, 4 secondary images, 2 lifestyle/ad variants, and a compliance checklist.
Charge enough to test seriousness, not just interest.
Track gross margin after GPT Image 2 cost, human review time, and revision count.

positioning.frequency: per new SKU, seasonal refresh, or marketplace rejection event
positioning.currentAlternative: freelancer, agency, ecommerce image editor, or generic AI image tool
positioning.deliverable: platform-ready SKU image pack plus compliance notes

recommendedModel.rationale: Manual service validates paid demand and creates before/after datasets before investing in full workflow automation.

pricing.servicePriceRange: $19-79 per SKU pack or RMB 99-399 per SKU pack
pricing.anchor: cost of outsourced image editing, time saved per SKU, and reduced rejection/revision risk

revenueProgression.zeroToOne: 2 paid SKU packs from real sellers
revenueProgression.oneToOneK: 20 paid SKU packs or 5 repeat sellers
revenueProgression.oneKToTenK: repeatable seller acquisition channel plus batch workflow
revenueProgression.killOrPivotThreshold: 0 paid SKU packs after 15 qualified seller conversations

cacAssumptions: manual outreach and community replies first
paid ads only after conversion is proven
ltvAssumptions: repeat sellers refresh images monthly or per launch
unitEconomics.paybackAssumption: payback must happen on first SKU pack in service phase

handoffSummary.summary: Build only a concierge workflow and checklist first; defer self-serve SaaS until 2 paid SKU packs and repeat demand are validated.
